votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, checking the length condition, the ordering of the result pair, the existence of the elements in the list at distinct indices, and the minimality of their absolute difference.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, checking the length condition, the ordering of the result pair, their presence at distinct indices in the list, and that their difference is less than or equal to the absolute difference of any pair of elements at distinct indices.

The Python code correctly implements all clauses of the Coq specification: it handles the length precondition, verifies a <= b, checks for the existence of a and b at distinct indices, and verifies that b - a is the minimum difference between any pair of distinct elements.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec: it returns True when len(numbers) < 2 (matching the Coq precondition that makes the implication vacuously true), checks a <= b, verifies that a and b exist at distinct indices in the list, and confirms that b - a is the minimum distance between any two distinct elements. The logic matches the Coq specification for all concrete inputs.
